question: drill pipeline applied to its own source/config/seeds — fixpoint, diverge, or paradox?
method: seed_id = short label; seed_text = sha256(file). Simulate runner v2 module_fallback (hash_proj 31-poly, 5 lenses: blowup/closure/gap/phi/self_ref). Run twice. η = absorption classifier state; θ = cosine+jaccard on per-lens hash signature (run1 vs run2).

outcome_table.DIVERGE: run1 state ≠ run2 state on any input (deterministic guarantee broken)
outcome_table.PARADOX: η=fixpoint on ≥1 input BUT cross-drift present (absorbed + diverge simultaneously)

implication: drill v2 is absorbed under self-application — the pipeline maps its own artefacts (runner source, criteria, seeds) to η=fixpoint with θ=1.0 (deterministic oracle: run2 collapses to run1). Self-reference is stable, not paradoxical: drill ∘ drill = drill.
caveat: Determinism of the oracle forces θ=1.0 by construction; a true paradox probe would require a non-deterministic perturbation layer (noise injection or real module dispatch with environment-dependent side effects). Under current v2 semantics, FIXPOINT is structurally guaranteed for any positive-kind self-input.
